task_completion.description: Patient and prescribing doctor are correctly selected in the prescription form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The prescription form shows the patient name as 'Ana Isabel Betz' (or 'Ana Betz') and the health professional/doctor as 'Cordara, Cameron'.
The medication or drug field contains 'Metformin'.

task_completion.description: The correct dose and route of administration are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dose field shows '500 mg' and the route field shows 'Oral'.

task_completion.description: The correct frequency and duration are entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The frequency field explicitly shows 'Twice daily (morning and evening)' and the duration field shows '30 days'.
The indication, condition, or reason field contains the text 'Type 1 Diabetes management - adjunct therapy'.

task_completion.description: The prescription is saved with today's date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The prescription record appears saved (e.g., a record ID is visible, save button is greyed out, or a success message is shown) and the prescription date matches the current date of the system.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the health information system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual GNU Health (or equivalent medical software) GUI being used, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not type the answers into a text editor or note-taking app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are located inside the designated form fields of the medical software's prescription module.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the form elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dropdowns, search dialogs, or auto-complete fields for patient, doctor, and medication appear to have been used, indicating interaction with the underlying database rather than just typing text into a blank canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target patient's full name in the database is 'Ana Isabel Betz'. The prescribing doctor is 'Cordara, Cameron'. The medication is 'Metformin'. The dose is '500 mg'. The route is 'Oral'. The frequency must explicitly be 'Twice daily (morning and evening)'. The duration is '30 days'. The indication is 'Type 1 Diabetes management - adjunct therapy'.
